title: Emergence of a correlated insulating state in bulk 1T-NbSe$_2$ via metal intercalation
authors: M. Tomlinson, AKM A. Rahman, S. Devi, R. Tuchikawa, M. Ishigami, D. Le, Md Z. Mohayman, A. Kushima, Y. Nakajima
abstract: The 1T polymorph of NbSe$_2$, long confined to the monolayer limit, has remained inaccessible in bulk. Here, we report the realization of bulk 1T-NbSe$_2$ via electrochemical Sn intercalation. Transmission electron microscopy directly reveals the formation of the 1T structure induced by Sn intercalation. The intercalated samples exhibit insulating transport behavior, in stark contrast to metallic 2H-NbSe$_2$. Density functional theory calculations, however, predict a metallic band structure, highlighting the crucial role of emergent electronic correlations in the observed insulating state. Raman spectroscopy further reveals vibrational modes associated with Sn intercalation and possible charge density wave order. Our results establish electrochemical intercalation as an effective route to stabilize otherwise inaccessible bulk polytypes, positioning bulk 1T-NbSe$_2$ as a new platform for investigating correlated electronic states.
summary: Researchers have used electrochemical intercalation to stabilize the previously inaccessible bulk 1T phase of NbSe2. This new material exhibits an insulating state that cannot be explained by standard DFT, suggesting that strong electronic correlations are at play. This provides a new 3D platform for studying correlated electronic phases.
main_problem: The 1T polymorph of NbSe2 has been historically inaccessible in bulk form, and the origin of the insulating state in the stabilized bulk 1T phase needs to be determined.
main_result: The authors successfully realized bulk 1T-NbSe2 via electrochemical Sn intercalation, revealing an insulating state driven by emergent electronic correlations rather than Anderson localization.
method: The study uses electrochemical Sn intercalation, XRD, TEM, Raman spectroscopy, and transport measurements, complemented by spin-polarized Density Functional Theory (DFT) calculations.
model_or_system: The physical system is bulk NbSe2 (a transition metal dichalcogenide) in the 1T (octahedral) phase, stabilized by Sn intercalation into the van der Waals gaps.
key_observables: Lattice expansion (c-axis), resistivity (temperature dependence), Sn concentration, and Raman vibrational modes.
important_parameters: Sn concentration (~6%), temperature-dependent resistivity, and the 1T/2H structural phase transition.
main_assumptions: The study assumes that the insulating behavior is not due to Anderson localization because the Sn intercalants couple weakly to in-plane motion.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows 2H and 1T coordination; Fig 4 displays c-axis stacking configurations and DFT-calculated band structures; Fig 5 compares Raman spectra of pristine and intercalated NbSe2.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of 1T-NbSe2 accessibility, describes the electrochemical synthesis and structural characterization, presents transport and spectroscopic evidence for the insulating state, and uses DFT to argue for the role of electronic correlations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Coordinations and crystal structures of (a) 2H-NbSe2 and (b) 1T-NbSe2. (c) XRD patterns of Sn-intercalated and pristine NbSe2 using a Cu Kα radiation. Inset: XRD patterns for Sn-intercalated and pristine NbSe2 centered around 14◦.

FIG. 3. Temperature dependence of the resistivity in the ab-plane for pristine and Sn-intercalated NbSe2. The inset displays low temperature resistivity of Sn-intercalated NbSe2 highlighting a transition at 7 K.

FIG. 2. (a) High-resolution TEM and (b) its inverse FFT im- ages for pristine and Sn-intercalated NbSe2. (c) Line proﬁles from regions (i) and (ii), highlighted in (a), corresponding to the 1T and 2H phases, respectively. Zoomed-in images of (d) the 1T phase and (e) the 2H phase, corresponding to the blue- and red-square regions in (b), respectively.

FIG. 5. Raman spectra for pristine and Sn-intercalated NbSe2. Arrows indicate peaks absent from the calculated Raman-active modes for pristine 1T-NbSe2.
